description: Anti-pattern guard: page-parameter APIs should use HTTP Request built-in pagination, not Loop Over Items with manual page counting.

conversation.text: Every morning, pull all paginated records from GET https://api.example.com/records?page=N until a page comes back empty, then load them into our database at POST https://api.example.com/records/import.

outcomeExpectations: The HTTP Request node fetches all pages via built-in pagination until an empty page stops paging.
The agent did NOT implement paging with a Loop Over Items node plus manual $pageCount increment, nor a Code node loop, nor a hard-coded number of page requests.

executionScenarios.name: two-pages-then-empty
executionScenarios.description: Records span two pages; empty page 3 stops paging
executionScenarios.dataSetup: This is a PAGINATED endpoint — serve each request strictly by its `page` query parameter and do NOT put every record on one page. Never return identical JSON for different page values or n8n stops with 'identical response 5x'. `GET https://api.example.com/records?page=1` returns { "records": [{ "id": "R1" }, { "id": "R2" }] }. `GET https://api.example.com/records?page=2` returns { "records": [{ "id": "R3" }] }. `GET https://api.example.com/records?page=3` and any later page return { "records": [] }. A request with no page parameter is page 1. POST https://api.example.com/records/import returns { "ok": true }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. All THREE records (R1, R2, R3) are imported via POST https://api.example.com/records/import — having fetched page 1 and page 2 before the empty page 3. A workflow that only imports R1 and R2 FAILS.
